outcome_contract.why: Guest portal personalization relies on the `?uuid=` query parameter in a fragile way that could cause data leakage or silent fallback to wrong guest data.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: All entry points to the guarded home pass uuid exclusively through the server-validated session; uuid tamper risk is eliminated; no guest can access another guest's data by manipulating the URL parameter.

engineering_coverage.applicable: Error state for transient context-null handled by localStorage fallback; no new user-facing state required
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Major UX regression risk (latency per read)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Context-null must degrade to localStorage fallback; `useUuid` returns localStorage uuid + emits telemetry when context is absent

engineering_coverage.applicable: Eliminates URL tamper vector; uuid sourced from server-validated session
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Eliminates URL tamper vector and SDK direct access entirely
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: uuid is authoritative from server; URL param is advisory only (used for deep link compatibility only, rejected if it mismatches context)

engineering_coverage.applicable: Add client-side event when URL uuid differs from context uuid (mismatch detection); retain server-side `console.warn` in `guest-booking.ts`
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: CF Function logs cover all data reads
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Mismatch analytics event must be added to `useUuid`; baseline measurement before release needed

engineering_coverage.applicable: `validateGuestToken` return type change requires mock updates (4 callers + tests); `AuthSessionContext` is straightforward to mock in tests; `useUuid` becomes testable as context consumer
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: All hooks need new test suites for CF endpoints
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: TASK-08 (tests) must follow TASK-04 (context); mock update burden is bounded and mechanical

engineering_coverage.applicable: `/api/guest-session` GET response contract extends (`guestUuid` added); `validateGuestToken` return type changes from `TokenValidationResult` string enum to `{status, guestUuid}` object
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Contract changes are additive; `TokenValidationResult` can be kept as a sub-field; backward-compatible if callers handle both shapes during migration

engineering_coverage.applicable: One additional field in `/api/guest-session` GET response — negligible; context is populated before children render (blocking at GuardedGate); no new network calls
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Massive latency increase (26+ edge function calls per page load)
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Degrade gracefully on transient GET failure: use localStorage uuid + telemetry; gate on `!!uuid` already protects hooks from empty uuid

engineering_coverage.applicable: No feature flag needed; code revert restores prior behavior; existing bookmarked `/?uuid=<id>` links work as long as localStorage uuid matches session uuid (always true post-login)
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Staged rollout possible per hook; high regression risk per hook
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Simple rollback by code revert; bookmarked links should continue to work via localStorage fallback path

open_operator_questions: What are the current Firebase RTDB security rules for `bookings/`, `guestsDetails/`, `loans/`?

stage_payload.decision_summary: The prime guest portal personalization mechanism uses a `?uuid=` URL query parameter (`occ_<13 digits>`) as the Firebase lookup key for all guest data. The authentication layer (`prime_session` HttpOnly cookie) validates session ownership but does not bind the uuid to the session at the client level. This creates a design gap: an authenticated guest can substitute any valid-format `occ_<id>` in the URL and — depending on Firebase RTDB rules — read another occupant's booking, financial, and personal data. Additionally, the `useFetchBookingsData` full-scan fallback sends the entire `bookings/` r
goals: Eliminate the url-param tamper vector for guest data access.
Bind uuid resolution to the server-validated session (`prime_session` cookie path).
Fix find-booking to issue tokens for the matched occupant, not the lead guest.
Clean up `GuestSessionSnapshot` type divergence and stale test mocks.
Add test coverage for uuid tamper, mismatch, and localStorage fallback.
non_goals: Rewriting Firebase RTDB security rules (out-of-codebase-scope; operator confirmation needed).
constraints: Cloudflare Pages Functions (CF Pages edge) — no server-side React rendering in the data-fetch path; context must be populated client-side after a GET to `/api/guest-session`.
26 `useUuid()` call sites must continue to work after the change; blast radius must be managed.
assumptions: Firebase RTDB rules are assumed open (API-key-only) given the `FUNCTION_ONLY` declared vs. actual SDK-direct gap.
The chosen approach must degrade gracefully if `/api/guest-session` fails transiently — guests must not be silently locked out due to a network blip.

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Core security goal — prevents cross-occupant data reads
Don't trade url tamper fragility for new session-context SPOF
`/?uuid=<id>` links may be bookmarked; breaking them is a UX regression
Hardening must be testable; context-based uuid is easier to mock than URL parsing
Separate risk but same threat surface
Fewer layers = easier to maintain and audit

options_considered.Description: Enhance GET to return `guestUuid`; update `validateGuestToken` to return `{status, guestUuid}`; store uuid in a new `AuthSessionContext` at `GuardedGate`; rewrite `useUuid` to read from context first, then localStorage fallback
options_considered.Upside: Authoritative server-sourced uuid; tamper vector eliminated; clean context API; test seam is straightforward to mock
options_considered.Downside: Changes `validateGuestToken` return type (4 callers + all mocks must update); adds one React context layer; requires `/api/guest-session` GET change
options_considered.Key risks: Context null on transient failure — must degrade to localStorage fallback with telemetry

options_considered.Option: B: Enforce FUNCTION_ONLY at hook level (route all data through CF Functions)
options_considered.Description: Rewrite data hooks to call CF Functions instead of Firebase SDK directly; CF Functions already validate session and cross-check `guestUuid`
options_considered.Upside: Enforces declared policy; eliminates SDK direct access entirely; no URL param matters when server controls all data access
options_considered.Downside: Massive blast radius: 26+ hooks, all need CF Function endpoints; massive latency increase (edge function per read vs. SDK); complete rewrite of data layer
options_considered.Key risks: Very high complexity, breaks phased loading (OPT-03), likely regression-prone; disproportionate to the risk
options_considered.Viable?: Yes (partial — use for new flows only)

options_considered.Option: C: UUID-lock in localStorage at first write, reject URL override
options_considered.Description: Change `useUuid` to always prefer `localStorage['prime_guest_uuid']` over URL; ignore URL uuid if localStorage has a value
options_considered.Upside: No new API call; no context layer; minimal blast radius
options_considered.Downside: Does not bind uuid to server-verified session — an attacker clears localStorage and loads uuid from URL; still tamper-able
options_considered.Key risks: Doesn't actually solve the security problem, only raises the bar slightly

options_considered.Option: D: Firebase RTDB per-occupant security rules only
options_considered.Description: Operator configures Firebase rules to enforce `auth.uid === occupantId` — no code change
options_considered.Upside: Solves the threat at the infrastructure layer
options_considered.Downside: Requires Firebase custom auth tokens per guest (the SDK connection is currently anonymous/API-key); major infrastructure change; not in codebase scope; blocks fast path
options_considered.Key risks: Requires Firebase custom auth token issuance — new auth flow entirely; incompatible with current CF-managed session model in short term
options_considered.Viable?: No — out of scope for this plan

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option A — extend `/api/guest-session` GET to return `guestUuid`, update `validateGuestToken` to return the session payload, populate `AuthSessionContext` at `GuardedGate`, and rewrite `useUuid` to read from context as primary source with localStorage as fallback.
chosen_approach.Directly and surgically addresses the se: uuid is now sourced from the server-validated session, not from an unverified URL parameter.
chosen_approach.Minimal blast radius compared to Option: 4 caller sites for `validateGuestToken` return type change, plus mechanical mock updates in tests. The 26 data-hook call sites are unchanged — `useUuid()` API surface stays the same.
chosen_approach.Clean degradation path: if `/api/guest-session` GET fails transiently (network error), `useUuid` falls back to `localStorage['prime_guest_uuid']` with an error telemetry event rather than returning empty string and breaking all 26 hooks silently. If context is null AND localStorage is also null (guest not yet authenticated), `useUuid` returns `null` and all downstream hooks remain gated on `!!uuid` — this is identical to today's not-yet-authenticated behavior.
chosen_approach.Backward-compatible: bookmarked `/?uuid=<id>` URLs continue to work because localStorage still holds the correct uuid post-login; the URL param becomes advisory-only for deep-link compatibility, overridden by context.
chosen_approach.`app/page.tsx` at root also calls `valid: ** Accept that `app/page.tsx` will continue to source uuid from localStorage (via `useUuid` localStorage fallback path). This is correct and safe: the session POST already wrote `guestUuid` to localStorage, so the uuid is accurate; the root page is not a bookmarked tamper entry point. The plan should add a code comment at `app/page.tsx` documenting this deliberate exclusion from `AuthSessionContext` coverage.
chosen_approach.**Option B (Full FUNCTION_ONLY enforceme: 26+ hooks require new CF endpoints, adding latency to every data read, and breaking phased loading. Appropriate as a long-term architecture direction for new flows, not as this fix.
chosen_approach.Q: What are the current Firebase RTDB security rules for `bookings/`, `guestsDetails/`, `loans/`?
chosen_approach.Why operator input is required: rules are configured in the Firebase console, not in the codebase.
chosen_approach.Planning impact: determines whether this fix is prioritized as critical (rules open/API-key-only → data exposure is real) or advisory (rules already enforce per-occupant reads → fix is defense-in-depth). The plan proceeds either way, but the operator should confirm urgency before setting deadline.

rejected_approaches: **Option B (Full FUNCTION_ONLY enforcement)** — Rejected. Correct in principle (it is the declared policy), but the blast radius is disproportionate: 26+ hooks require new CF endpoints, adding latency to every data read, and breaking phased loading. Appropriate as a long-term architecture direction for new flows, not as this fix.

**Option C (localStorage-first with URL rejected)** — Rejected. Does not bind uuid to the server session. An attacker who clears localStorage and navigates with `?uuid=<target>` is still unimpeded. Not a security fix.

**Option D (Firebase per-occupant rules)** — Rejected for this plan. Requires Firebase custom auth token issuance — a significant infrastructure change incompatible with the current CF-managed session model. Track as a separate hardening item.

validation_implications: All existing tests for `GuardedGate`, `portal/page.tsx`, and `useSessionValidation` require mock updates when `validateGuestToken` return type changes from string to object. These must be updated before or simultaneously with the API contract change to avoid CI breakage.
New tests for `useUuid` context path, localStorage fallback, null-both-absent, and mismatch detection are required before the artifact is complete.
The `/api/guest-session` GET endpoint needs a contract test confirming the `guestUuid` field is present in the response.
sequencing_constraints: API contract extension must precede the context layer (context population needs the payload shape).
Context layer must be complete before `useUuid` tests can accurately cover the context source path.
risks_to_carry_forward: `fetchViaFullScan` passive full-scan exposure is pre-existing and not addressed in this change set — planning must note it as a follow-up item requiring a separate feature slug.
`/api/assistant-query` endpoint auth model is unconfirmed — inspect before scoping the `digital-assistant` fix. If it requires an endpoint auth migration, scope and effort increase.

areas.label: Deep link entry
areas.current: GET `/api/guest-session?token=<token>` returns `{status, expiresAt}`; client writes uuid to localStorage
areas.future: Unchanged — token verification still writes `bookingId`, `guestUuid`, `firstName`, `verifiedAt` to localStorage after POST `/api/guest-session`

areas.label: GuardedGate (auth check)
areas.steady: 'network_error'`; sets `gateState='allowed'` on valid; no uuid propagated
areas.seams: Guest navigates to any route under `(guarded)/`

areas.label: uuid resolution in data hooks
areas.current: `useUuid()` reads `?uuid` from URL; falls back to localStorage; validates format
areas.future: `useUuid()` reads `AuthSessionContext` first (server-confirmed uuid). If context is absent (transient failure), falls back to `localStorage['prime_guest_uuid']`. URL `?uuid` param is no longer an override — it is only used for initial deep-link routing compatibility (matching URL to stored session). If URL uuid differs from context uuid, a mismatch analytics event is emitted and context uuid wins.
areas.steady: Firebase lookup logic (reverse index + full scan); React Query caching; all 26 hook call sites
areas.seams: `app/page.tsx` is outside GuardedLayout so it has no `AuthSessionContext` — by design; `useUuid` localStorage fallback provides the correct uuid

areas.label: find-booking token issuance
areas.future: Token issued for `matchedOccupantId`. If matched occupant is the lead, behavior is identical to current. Non-lead guests now get a session scoped to their own uuid, not the lead's.
areas.steady: Rate limiting; surname matching logic; redirect to `/g/<token>`
areas.seams: One edge case: if a booking has a single occupant who is both lead and matched, behavior is unchanged

areas.label: `digital-assistant` stale token read
areas.current: Reads `localStorage.getItem('prime_guest_token')` — always `null` under current flow; sends `null` to `/api/assistant-query`
areas.future: Remove `prime_guest_token` read; use cookie-based auth path (the assistant API should use `prime_session` cookie like all other endpoints)
areas.seams: `/api/assistant-query` endpoint must accept `prime_session` cookie instead of body `token` field; or the `token` field can be dropped from the request body if the endpoint validates via cookie

areas.label: Type / test cleanup
areas.current: `GuestSessionSnapshot` lacks `token` field; tests mock it with stale shape; `useSessionValidation` test references `token` param that doesn't exist on the hook
areas.future: `GuestSessionSnapshot` type, test mocks, and `useSessionValidation` tests updated to match current implementation; no `token` param in hook or type
areas.steady: Behavior unchanged; only types and mocks cleaned up
areas.seams: Test suite must pass after type cleanup; no behavioral regressions
